assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been recognised as a foreign-currency monetary receivable/payable rather than remaining a forecast transaction.
The receivable/payable is between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised asset, and IFRS 9 allows hedge accounting in consolidation for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a distinct cash flow hedge model for foreign currency risk associated with recognised assets or liabilities, which is relevant to a foreign-currency intragroup receivable.
